wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the TaxHacker project overview section. Explain the project's purpose as a self-hosted AI accounting application for freelancers, indie-hackers, and small businesses. Document the core value proposition of automating expense and income tracking using modern AI. Describe the key features including AI-powered document analysis, multi-currency support, customizable categories and fields, and self-hosted deployment options. Include the target audience, use cases, and positioning in the market. Provide architectural overview showing the Next.js 15 frontend, TypeScript backend, AI processing engine, and PostgreSQL database. Document the technology stack and highlight the self-hosted vs cloud deployment options. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include practical examples of common workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the LLM provider integration system. Detail the unified provider abstraction that supports OpenAI, Google Gemini, Mistral AI, and local LLMs. Explain the provider selection logic, configuration management, and fallback mechanisms. Document the requestLLM function, provider initialization, and authentication handling. Include configuration examples for each supported provider, API key management, and rate limiting strategies. Explain token usage tracking, cost calculation, and provider-specific features. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common provider connectivity issues and error handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Mechanisms
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for authentication mechanisms in TaxHacker. Explain the Better Auth integration with email OTP authentication system, including the dual authentication modes: cloud-based with Better Auth and self-hosted mode with auto-login capabilities. Document the email OTP plugin configuration, OTP length, expiration times, and verification process. Detail the authentication flow from user registration through login, including passwordless email authentication, OTP verification, and session establishment. Explain the difference between cloud and self-hosted authentication modes, including configuration requirements and security implications. Document the authentication API endpoints, request/response patterns, and error handling. Include practical examples of implementing authentication in Next.js app router with server actions and client components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Data Model
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Transaction entity in TaxHacker. Detail the transaction schema including all fields (name, description, merchant, total, currencyCode, convertedTotal, type, note, files, extra fields), data types, and validation rules. Document the relationship with Category and Project entities through categoryCode and projectCode foreign keys. Explain the TransactionData interface structure, optional vs required fields, and the splitTransactionDataExtraFields mechanism for dynamic field handling. Include field constraints, indexing strategies, and data integrity requirements. Document the extra field system for custom transaction attributes and how it integrates with the flexible field system. Provide examples of transaction data structures and explain the items array for transaction splitting functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for TaxHacker's authentication endpoints. Document the complete authentication flow including email OTP verification, session management, and Better Auth integration. For the main authentication endpoint (/api/auth/[...all]), specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Document login/logout procedures, session token handling, and JWT management. Include error response formats for authentication failures, invalid credentials, and expired sessions. Provide client implementation examples showing proper authentication flow, token storage, and session renewal. Document security considerations including CSRF protection, rate limiting, and secure cookie handling. Include troubleshooting guides for common authentication issues and debugging approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for TaxHacker. Include detailed installation and setup instructions for both self-hosted and cloud deployment options. Document environment variable configuration including UPLOAD_PATH, DATABASE_URL, SELF_HOSTED_MODE, and BETTER_AUTH_SECRET. Provide step-by-step local development setup with Node.js, PostgreSQL, and dependency installation. Include Docker Compose configuration examples and custom deployment scenarios. Document the initial application configuration, database initialization with Prisma migrations, and first-time user onboarding. Provide troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and environment configuration problems. Include verification steps to ensure proper installation and connectivity to the database.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the file processing pipeline that handles document ingestion and preparation for AI analysis. Explain the attachment processing workflow from file upload through format detection to AI-ready content preparation. Document support for PDFs, images, and various document formats. Detail the file conversion process, image enhancement, and text extraction capabilities. Explain preview generation, caching strategies, and storage management. Include file size limitations, supported formats, and processing time considerations. Provide examples of successful file processing scenarios and troubleshooting guidance for common format issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive session management documentation for TaxHacker. Detail the JWT-based session strategy with 365-day expiration and 24-hour update age settings. Explain session persistence through cookie caching with 365-day max age, including cookie prefix configuration and secure cookie handling. Document the session retrieval mechanisms, including getSession() and getCurrentUser() functions, and their behavior in both cloud and self-hosted modes. Explain middleware implementation for protecting routes, session validation, and automatic redirects based on authentication state. Detail session storage, cookie management, and cross-browser compatibility considerations. Include troubleshooting guidance for session-related issues, expired sessions, and authentication state problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for transaction CRUD operations in TaxHacker. Document the complete transaction lifecycle including createTransaction, updateTransaction, deleteTransaction, and bulkDeleteTransactions functions. Explain transaction creation from AI-extracted data, manual entry processes, and transaction splitting functionality. Detail the splitTransactionDataExtraFields mechanism for handling dynamic fields and the standard vs extra field separation. Document transaction retrieval methods including getTransactionById and getTransactionsByFileId. Explain transaction updates with partial field modifications and file attachment management. Include error handling strategies, validation rules, and transaction state management. Provide code examples for each operation and explain the integration with the UI components for transaction management.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for TaxHacker's file management endpoints. Document file upload endpoints with multipart form data handling, supported file formats (PDF, images, documents), and size limitations. For file download endpoints (/files/download[fileId]), specify URL patterns, authentication requirements, and response formats. Document preview generation endpoints (/files/preview[fileId]) with image processing and caching mechanisms. Include static file serving endpoints (/files/static[filename]) for public assets. Document file metadata handling, storage quotas, and cleanup policies. Provide client implementation examples for file upload workflows, progress tracking, and error handling. Include file validation rules, security considerations for file uploads, and performance optimization tips for large file handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and user management documentation for TaxHacker. Explain the Better Auth integration with email OTP authentication system. Document the dual authentication modes: cloud-based with Better Auth and self-hosted mode with auto-login capabilities. Detail user registration, login workflows, session management, and JWT token handling. Document the authentication guards, protected routes, and middleware implementation. Explain user roles, permissions, and feature access based on subscription status. Include passwordless email authentication flow, OTP verification process, and session persistence. Document the self-hosted configuration options, custom API key management, and security considerations. Provide troubleshooting guidance for authentication issues and session problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Prompt Engineering System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AI prompt engineering system that drives data extraction accuracy. Explain the prompt construction process, template system, and schema-driven extraction patterns. Document the relationship between field definitions and prompt templates. Detail prompt customization options, dynamic parameter injection, and context building. Explain schema validation, data type enforcement, and extraction rule configuration. Include examples of effective prompts for different document types, prompt optimization techniques, and debugging approaches for extraction failures. Provide guidelines for creating custom prompts and extending the schema system.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for user profile management in TaxHacker. Document the UserProfile interface and User model structure, including fields for membership plan, storage usage, storage limits, and AI balance tracking. Explain user data retrieval functions, profile updates, and data validation processes. Detail the user profile form components, including profile settings management and data binding. Document user role-based access control, subscription status handling, and feature limitations based on membership tiers. Explain user data privacy, GDPR compliance considerations, and data retention policies. Include examples of user profile operations, form validation, and error handling for profile management tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Filtering and Search
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for transaction filtering and search capabilities in TaxHacker. Document the TransactionFilters interface including search terms, date ranges (dateFrom/dateTo), category filtering (categoryCode), project filtering (projectCode), transaction type filtering (type), ordering options (ascending/descending), and pagination parameters (page). Explain the dynamic query building process that constructs Prisma queries based on filter combinations. Detail the multi-field search functionality that searches across name, merchant, description, note, and text fields with case-insensitive matching. Document the ordering system supporting multiple fields with prefix notation for direction. Include examples of complex filter combinations and performance considerations for large transaction datasets. Explain the integration with the frontend filter components and pagination system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for TaxHacker's Stripe payment processing endpoints. Document checkout session creation (/api/stripe/checkout) with subscription plans, pricing tiers, and success/failure callbacks. For customer portal endpoints (/api/stripe/portal), specify billing management, subscription updates, and payment method changes. Document webhook handling (/api/stripe/webhook) for asynchronous payment events, subscription status updates, and failure notifications. Include request/response schemas for each endpoint, authentication requirements, and security considerations. Provide client implementation examples showing checkout flow integration, webhook verification, and error handling strategies. Document subscription lifecycle management, usage tracking, and refund processing. Include debugging approaches for payment failures and webhook troubleshooting.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Processing Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive AI processing engine documentation for TaxHacker's automated document analysis system. Explain the multi-LLM provider architecture supporting OpenAI, Google Gemini, Mistral AI, and local LLMs. Document the AI analysis pipeline from file upload through data extraction to transaction creation. Detail the prompt engineering system, schema validation, and data transformation processes. Explain token usage tracking, cost management, and provider selection logic. Document the file attachment processing workflow, image recognition, and PDF parsing capabilities. Include configuration options for different LLM providers, custom prompt templates, and extraction rules. Provide examples of successful AI extractions and troubleshooting guidance for common processing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analysis Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the complete AI analysis workflow from initial file upload through final transaction creation. Explain the analyzeTransaction function orchestration, state management, and error handling. Detail the integration with file models, settings management, and result caching. Document the ActionState pattern, success/error handling, and data transformation processes. Explain the relationship between analysis results and subsequent transaction creation. Include workflow diagrams, state transitions, and integration points with the broader system. Provide examples of complete analysis pipelines and troubleshooting guidance for workflow failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security documentation for TaxHacker authentication system. Detail the security measures including JWT token security, cookie security settings, and CSRF protection. Explain the self-hosted security configuration, custom API key management, and security considerations for local deployments. Document passwordless authentication security, OTP verification security, and session hijacking prevention. Detail middleware-based route protection, authentication guards, and unauthorized access handling. Explain security headers, HTTPS enforcement, and secure cookie attributes. Include security best practices, vulnerability mitigation strategies, and security audit considerations. Document the security implications of different authentication modes and provide guidance for securing production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Currency Support
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for multi-currency transaction support in TaxHacker. Explain the dual currency system with original currencyCode and convertedCurrencyCode fields, including automatic currency conversion logic during transaction processing. Document the currency conversion workflow triggered by AI processing and manual entry scenarios. Detail the currency conversion API integration and real-time exchange rate handling. Explain how convertedTotal is calculated and stored for display purposes while maintaining original transaction amounts. Document the currency converter agent functionality and user interface components for currency selection. Include examples of multi-currency transaction workflows, conversion rate management, and currency formatting for international users. Address currency precision handling, rounding strategies, and multi-currency reporting capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Export API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for TaxHacker's data export endpoints. Document transaction export functionality (/export/transactions) with supported formats (CSV, Excel, PDF), filtering options, and date range parameters. Specify request parameters including transaction IDs, date ranges, categories, and export formats. Document response formats, file generation processes, and download mechanisms. Include authentication requirements, rate limiting, and export size limitations. Provide client implementation examples showing export workflow integration, progress tracking, and error handling. Document data privacy considerations, export data validation, and performance optimization for large datasets. Include troubleshooting guides for export failures and data formatting issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive transaction management system documentation for TaxHacker. Document the transaction data model including fields, relationships, and validation rules. Explain CRUD operations for financial records, multi-currency support with automatic conversion, and category/project associations. Detail the advanced filtering and search capabilities, bulk operations, and transaction editing workflows. Document the transaction creation from AI-extracted data, manual entry processes, and transaction splitting functionality. Include transaction status management, approval workflows, and audit trails. Explain the integration with the AI processing engine and file attachment system. Provide examples of transaction workflows and troubleshooting guidance for common transaction management issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Transaction Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AI-powered transaction creation and integration in TaxHacker. Document how AI-extracted transaction data flows from the analyze.ts pipeline into the transaction creation process. Explain the data transformation from AI output to TransactionData format, including field mapping and validation. Detail the splitTransactionDataExtraFields mechanism for handling AI-generated dynamic fields. Document the transaction creation workflow from unsorted files to processed transactions, including the analyze-all-button functionality. Explain error handling for AI processing failures and manual correction workflows. Include examples of AI transaction creation, field validation rules, and integration with the file processing pipeline. Address AI provider configuration, token usage tracking, and performance optimization for batch transaction processing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for TaxHacker's utility endpoints. Document currency conversion endpoints (/api/currency) with supported currencies, exchange rates, and real-time updates. For progress tracking endpoints (/api/progress[progressId]), specify progress monitoring, status updates, and completion notifications. Include utility functions for date formatting, data validation, and system configuration. Document request/response schemas, caching strategies, and performance considerations. Provide client implementation examples for currency conversion workflows, progress monitoring, and utility function usage. Include error handling patterns, fallback mechanisms, and debugging approaches for utility endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Invoicing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive invoicing system documentation for TaxHacker's invoice generation and management capabilities. Document the invoice creation workflow from transaction selection to PDF generation. Explain the template system with default templates and custom field integration. Detail the invoice data mapping from transactions, customer information, and business settings. Document the PDF export functionality, styling options, and branding capabilities. Explain the invoice status management, send workflows, and client notification system. Include customization options for invoice fields, tax calculations, and payment terms. Document the integration with the transaction system and file attachment capabilities. Provide examples of invoice workflows and troubleshooting guidance for common invoicing issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Management & Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive file management and processing documentation for TaxHacker's document handling capabilities. Document the multi-format file upload system supporting PDF, images, and various document types. Explain the file processing pipeline including preview generation, thumbnail creation, and storage management. Detail the file validation, size limits, and security scanning processes. Document the file preview system, download functionality, and temporary file handling. Explain the integration with the AI processing engine and transaction attachment workflows. Include storage quota management, cleanup procedures, and backup considerations. Document the file metadata handling, indexing, and search capabilities. Provide troubleshooting guidance for file upload issues and processing errors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema & Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema and models documentation for TaxHacker's data architecture. Document the complete entity relationship diagram including Users, Transactions, Files, Categories, Projects, and AppData models. Detail field definitions, data types, constraints, and validation rules for each model. Explain primary and foreign key relationships, indexes, and performance optimizations. Document the Prisma schema configuration, migration management, and database seeding processes. Include data integrity constraints, cascading rules, and referential integrity. Explain the database initialization process, migration execution, and schema evolution strategies. Document data access patterns, query optimization, and caching strategies. Provide examples of common queries and troubleshooting guidance for database issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive settings and configuration documentation for TaxHacker's customization capabilities. Document the application-wide settings including business information, currency configuration, and feature toggles. Explain user-specific preferences, notification settings, and personalization options. Detail the business settings including company information, tax details, and regional configurations. Document the custom field system for extending data capture beyond standard fields. Explain category and project management including creation, modification, and deletion workflows. Include field customization options, validation rules, and display preferences. Document the settings import/export functionality and backup procedures. Provide guidance on optimal configuration strategies and troubleshooting common settings issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payment Processing & Billing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive payment processing and billing documentation for TaxHacker's subscription management system. Document the Stripe integration including checkout sessions, subscription management, and payment processing workflows. Explain the billing cycle, recurring payments, and proration handling. Detail the subscription tiers, feature access controls, and usage-based billing. Document the Stripe webhook handling for payment events, subscription updates, and failed payments. Include customer portal functionality, invoice generation, and payment history tracking. Explain the integration between billing and authentication systems for feature gating. Document the self-hosted billing configuration and cloud deployment billing options. Provide troubleshooting guidance for payment processing issues and subscription management problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Email System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive email system documentation for TaxHacker's transactional and marketing email capabilities. Document the email configuration including SMTP settings, provider integration, and authentication requirements. Explain the email templates for user authentication, password reset, OTP verification, and newsletter subscriptions. Detail the email sending workflows, queue management, and delivery tracking. Document the email content customization, branding options, and localization support. Include the integration with authentication flows, user onboarding sequences, and administrative notifications. Explain email security measures, spam prevention, and deliverability optimization. Document the email analytics, bounce handling, and retry mechanisms. Provide troubleshooting guidance for email delivery issues and configuration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components & Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive UI components and design system documentation for TaxHacker's React component library. Document the reusable UI primitives including buttons, inputs, dialogs, and navigation components. Explain the form components with validation, error handling, and accessibility features. Detail the dashboard widgets, charts, and data visualization components. Document the sidebar navigation, mobile menu, and responsive design patterns. Include the design system including color schemes, typography, spacing, and component variants. Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and customization options. Document the Tailwind CSS integration, theme management, and dark/light mode support. Provide usage examples, accessibility guidelines, and contribution standards for component development.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation for TaxHacker's REST API endpoints. Document all public API routes including authentication endpoints (/api/auth/[...all]), file upload and processing endpoints, payment processing endpoints (/api/stripe/), and data export endpoints. For each endpoint, specify HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Document the server actions pattern used for Next.js 13+ data operations. Include file upload APIs with multipart form data handling, progress tracking endpoints, and download functionality. Explain the API versioning strategy, rate limiting, and error response formats. Document authentication headers, session management, and security considerations. Provide client implementation examples, SDK usage patterns, and integration guidelines. Include API testing strategies and debugging approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation for TaxHacker's infrastructure requirements and deployment strategies. Document the Docker containerization approach including multi-stage builds, environment configuration, and volume management. Explain the production deployment with Docker Compose, Nginx reverse proxy configuration, and SSL termination. Detail the self-hosted deployment options, infrastructure requirements, and scaling considerations. Document the CI/CD pipeline, automated testing, and release management processes. Include monitoring setup with Sentry, health checks, and logging configuration. Explain backup strategies, disaster recovery procedures, and data migration processes. Document the environment variable management, secrets handling, and security hardening practices. Provide troubleshooting guidance for deployment issues and performance optimization tips.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines documentation for TaxHacker's coding standards and development practices. Document the TypeScript configuration, ESLint rules, and Prettier formatting standards. Explain the project structure conventions, file naming patterns, and directory organization principles. Detail the component development guidelines, React patterns, and Next.js best practices. Document the testing strategy including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end testing approaches. Explain the code review process, contribution guidelines, and pull request standards. Include debugging techniques, performance optimization practices, and profiling tools. Document the development environment setup, IDE configuration, and debugging workflows. Provide guidelines for maintaining code quality, security practices, and accessibility compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for TaxHacker's common issues and solutions. Document frequently encountered problems including authentication failures, file upload issues, AI processing errors, and database connectivity problems. Provide step-by-step diagnostic procedures for identifying root causes and resolution strategies. Include error code references, log analysis techniques, and debugging workflows. Document performance troubleshooting including slow queries, memory usage, and processing bottlenecks. Explain migration issues, version upgrade problems, and data consistency concerns. Include environment-specific troubleshooting for Docker deployments, cloud hosting, and self-hosted installations. Document security issues, permission problems, and configuration errors. Provide escalation procedures and community support resources.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# NalogManager: Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
NalogManager (also known as TaxHacker) is a modern financial management and invoicing application designed to streamline accounting processes through AI-powered automation. The platform serves as a personal AI accountant that helps users manage transactions, generate invoices, and maintain organized financial records.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **AI-Powered Financial Analysis**: Leverage multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Google Gemini, Mistral) for automated invoice parsing and transaction categorization
- **Multi-Platform Support**: Offer both cloud-hosted and self-hosted deployment options
- **Comprehensive Financial Management**: Provide tools for transaction tracking, invoicing, and financial reporting
- **Flexible Configuration**: Support customizable fields, categories, and business configurations

### Target Audience
- Small business owners and freelancers seeking automated financial management
- Accountants and bookkeepers looking for AI-assisted invoice processing
- Self-hosted users who prefer local data control
- Cloud users requiring scalable financial management solutions

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js Application] --> B[Authentication Layer]
    A --> C[AI Processing Engine]
    A --> D[Transaction Management]
    A --> E[File Upload & Processing]
    A --> F[Invoicing System]
    
    B --> G[Better Auth]
    C --> H[LangChain Providers]
    D --> I[Prisma ORM]
    E --> J[PDF/Image Processing]
    F --> K[React PDF Renderer]
    
    I --> L[PostgreSQL Database]
    H --> M[External LLM APIs]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Server Actions Pattern**: Modern Next.js 13+ server actions for secure data operations
- **Repository Pattern**: Clean separation between data access and business logic
- **Provider Pattern**: Modular AI provider abstraction supporting multiple LLM services
- **Plugin Architecture**: Extensible system for additional features and integrations

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User Interface
    participant S as Server Actions
    participant A as AI Provider
    participant DB as Database
    
    U->>S: Upload Invoice
    S->>A: Process with LLM
    A->>A: Extract Transaction Data
    A-->>S: Structured Results
    S->>DB: Store Transaction
    DB-->>U: Confirmation
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Layout**: `app/layout.tsx` - Global application configuration and metadata
- **Authenticated Layout**: `app/(app)/layout.tsx` - Protected routes with sidebar navigation
- **API Routes**: `app/api/` - RESTful endpoints for authentication, payments, and system operations

### Core Modules
- **Database Schema**: `prisma/schema.prisma` - Comprehensive financial data model with users, transactions, categories, and files
- **Configuration Management**: `lib/config.ts` - Environment-based configuration with validation
- **Authentication**: `lib/auth.ts` - Better Auth integration with email OTP and JWT sessions
- **AI Processing**: `ai/` - Multi-provider LLM integration with OpenAI, Google, and Mistral

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: Centralized configuration through Zod validation
- **Deployment Modes**: Self-hosted vs cloud-hosted with automatic feature toggles
- **Feature Flags**: Dynamic capability management based on subscription plans

### External Dependencies
- **Frontend Framework**: Next.js 15 with React 19 for modern React features
- **Database**: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM for type-safe database operations
- **AI Services**: LangChain integration with multiple LLM providers
- **Payment Processing**: Stripe integration for subscription management
- **Email Services**: Resend for transactional email delivery

### Integration Points
- **Stripe Webhooks**: `app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts` - Automated subscription management
- **File Processing**: PDF/image conversion with Ghostscript and GraphicsMagick
- **Email Notifications**: Real-time user notifications and OTP delivery

### Component Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Components] --> B[UI Library]
    A --> C[Forms]
    A --> D[Transactions]
    
    E[Models] --> F[Data Access]
    E --> G[Business Logic]
    
    H[Libraries] --> I[Utilities]
    H --> J[Services]
    
    A --> E
    E --> H
    F --> I
    J --> K[External APIs]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### AI-Powered Invoice Processing
- **Multi-Provider Support**: Seamless integration with OpenAI, Google Gemini, and Mistral AI
- **Automated Parsing**: Intelligent extraction of transaction data from various invoice formats
- **Schema Validation**: Structured data output with configurable field mappings

#### Comprehensive Transaction Management
- **Advanced Filtering**: Multi-dimensional filtering by date, category, project, and amount
- **Bulk Operations**: Efficient batch processing for large datasets
- **Real-time Updates**: Live transaction list with instant synchronization

#### Flexible Invoicing System
- **Template Management**: Customizable invoice templates with business branding
- **Multi-Currency Support**: Automatic currency conversion and multi-currency transactions
- **Export Capabilities**: PDF generation and CSV export functionality

### Implementation Highlights

#### AI Analysis Pipeline
- **File Processing**: `ai/analyze.ts` - Server-side AI analysis with token tracking
- **Provider Abstraction**: `ai/providers/llmProvider.ts` - Unified interface for multiple AI services
- **Attachment Handling**: `ai/attachments.ts` - Multi-format file processing (PDF, images, documents)

#### Transaction Management
- **Data Models**: `models/transactions.ts` - Comprehensive CRUD operations with caching
- **List Rendering**: `components/transactions/list.tsx` - Advanced table with sorting and filtering
- **Bulk Operations**: `components/transactions/bulk-actions.tsx` - Efficient batch processing

#### Authentication & Authorization
- **Session Management**: `lib/auth.ts` - Secure JWT-based authentication with email OTP
- **Subscription Control**: Automatic plan enforcement and feature gating
- **Self-Hosted Mode**: Local-only operation without external dependencies

### Feature Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login Success
    Authenticated --> Processing: Upload Invoice
    Processing --> AnalysisComplete: AI Analysis
    AnalysisComplete --> TransactionCreated: Save Data
    TransactionCreated --> Dashboard: Display Results
    Dashboard --> Processing: Upload More Files
    Dashboard --> Settings: Configure Options
    Settings --> Dashboard: Return to App
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
NalogManager demonstrates a well-architected full-stack application with clear separation of concerns, robust authentication, and scalable AI integration. The modular design allows for easy extension while maintaining performance and security standards.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Modular AI Integration**: Clean abstraction layer for multiple LLM providers
- **Type Safety**: Comprehensive TypeScript implementation with Prisma schemas
- **Performance Optimization**: React Server Components and caching strategies
- **Deployment Flexibility**: Both cloud and self-hosted deployment options

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Error Handling**: Enhanced error boundaries and user feedback mechanisms
- **Testing Coverage**: Expanded unit and integration test suites
- **Monitoring**: Additional observability with structured logging and metrics
- **Documentation**: Inline code documentation for complex AI processing flows

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Comprehensive Testing**: Add Jest/React Testing Library for critical components
2. **Enhance Monitoring**: Integrate Sentry for production error tracking and performance monitoring
3. **Optimize AI Costs**: Implement rate limiting and cost tracking for LLM usage
4. **Expand Documentation**: Create developer documentation for AI provider integration

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Mobile Application**: Consider React Native wrapper for mobile accessibility
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement real-time financial dashboards and reporting
- **Third-party Integrations**: API for accounting software and banking integrations
- **Multi-language Support**: Internationalization for global user base expansion

Sources:
- [package.json](package.json)
- [next.config.ts](next.config.ts)
- [lib/config.ts](lib/config.ts)
- [app/layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [app/(app)/layout.tsx](app/(app)/layout.tsx)
- [ai/analyze.ts](ai/analyze.ts)
- [models/transactions.ts](models/transactions.ts)
- [components/transactions/list.tsx](components/transactions/list.tsx)
- [app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts](app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts)
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile)
- [lib/auth.ts](lib/auth.ts)
- [app/(app)/apps/invoices/page.tsx](app/(app)/apps/invoices/page.tsx)

</blog>
